name: First State Bank Of Golva
description_short: Community bank with 100+ years of service across North Dakota locations, offering personal and business accounts, loans, credit cards, and fraud protection tools.
description_long:
First State Bank of Golva, Beach, and Medora is a regional community bank celebrating over a century of continuous service since 1914. The institution operates multiple branch locations across North Dakota communities and emphasizes long-standing relationships with local customers. Their core philosophy centers on customer-first decision-making, reflected in their statement: "If it's not good for our customers, it's not good for the Bank." This suggests a traditional community banking model prioritizing local accountability over corporate-wide metrics.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services. Personal offerings include checking and savings accounts, personal loans, credit cards, and mobile banking with debit alerts. Business customers can access business accounts, business financing, and loan products. The bank provides fraud prevention features including transaction alerts via text message, mobile debit card notifications, and educational content on common scams such as bank impersonation fraud and check washing schemes.

First State Bank distinguishes itself primarily through its deep community roots and longevity. The 100-year operational history in the same geographic region suggests established relationships with local customers and businesses. Their emphasis on fraud education and proactive alert systems indicates investment in customer security awareness, particularly relevant given the bank's focus on preventing mail theft and check fraud targeting rural areas.

As a small regional bank, First State Bank likely has more limited product sophistication and geographic reach compared to large national banks. The website provides minimal detail on account features, fee structures, interest rates, loan terms, or digital banking capabilities. Customers seeking comprehensive online banking, extensive branch networks, or specialized financial products may need to contact the bank directly for detailed comparisons with larger competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile debit card alerts via text notification
Multiple ATM locations across service areas
In-person customer service at branch locations
pros: 100+ years of continuous community service and local relationships since 1914
Multiple branch locations across North Dakota (Golva, Beach, Medora areas)
Mobile debit alerts and fraud transaction notifications via text message
Proactive fraud education including bank impersonation scams and check washing prevention
Personal and business loan products available
Credit card offerings with in-person application process
Multiple account types for personal and business customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to North Dakota communities only
Minimal online information about account features, fees, interest rates, or loan terms
Website provides no details on digital banking capabilities or mobile app functionality
Small regional bank may lack specialized products or services available at larger institutions
No transparent pricing or product comparison information available on website
best_for: North Dakota residents and businesses seeking community-focused local banking relationships
Small business owners in rural North Dakota communities needing personalized lending services
Customers prioritizing fraud prevention and security alert features
Long-term community members valuing institutional stability and local accountability
diagnosis: First State Bank of Golva is best suited for North Dakota residents and small businesses already embedded in or relocating to communities served by the bank (Golva, Beach, Medora areas) who value long-standing community relationships and personalized service. The primary caveat is the lack of transparent online information about products, pricing, and digital capabilities—customers must contact branches directly for detailed comparisons and feature specifications.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
